elevator_encounters_cornell.title: Long Way Down Cornell Notes
elevator_encounters_cornell.subtitle: Track elevator encounters, pressure, and choice
elevator_encounters_cornell.learning_target: I can track how structure and encounter moments shape my understanding of Will.
elevator_encounters_cornell.directions: Use page references or short evidence notes. Do not copy long passages from the novel.
elevator_encounters_cornell.summary_prompt: How did today's section change your understanding of Will?
elevator_encounters_cornell.reflection_prompt: What question are you carrying into the next section?

guided_reading_notes.title: Long Way Down Guided Reading Notes
guided_reading_notes.subtitle: Structure, repetition, memory, rules, and choice
guided_reading_notes.learning_target: I can notice how the novel's structure shapes tension and meaning.
guided_reading_notes.directions: Use brief notes, page references, or short evidence notes. Avoid copying long passages from the book.
prompts: What words, ideas, or patterns repeat?
Which rule or belief is present?
What choice is becoming clearer or harder?
success_criteria: I connected a moment to Will's internal conflict.
I used page references or brief evidence notes instead of long copied text.

discussion_notes.title: Long Way Down Discussion Notes
discussion_notes.subtitle: Track ideas from discussion and how your thinking changes
discussion_notes.learning_target: I can listen to discussion and record how my thinking develops.
discussion_notes.directions: Use the left column for a classmate's idea, question, or evidence note. Use the right column for your response or changed thinking.
discussion_notes.summary_prompt: What idea from discussion changed or sharpened your thinking?
